QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_19

question.text: What does "liquidity" mean in the context of mutual fund investments?

answer.text: The ease with which you can convert your mutual fund investment into cash
The amount of cash the fund manager keeps in the fund
The total assets managed by the fund
The interest rate earned on the investment

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Liquidity refers to how quickly and easily you can convert your investment back into cash. Open-ended mutual funds offer high liquidity because you can redeem your units on any business day. The redemption money typically reaches your bank account within 1-3 business days (for liquid funds, even within 1 day). **The Trap:** Liquidity is not about how much cash the fund holds or the total fund size — it is about how easily YOU can get your money back when you need it. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under features of mutual funds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Liquidity is a major advantage of mutual funds over investments like real estate or gold jewellery. Highlight this to clients: "If you need money urgently, you can get it within 1-3 days from most mutual funds."
